workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Can you work with me on pipeline hygiene for Alpine Ski House? I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-01-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:05:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:13:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:18:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-L trap (Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-L failure mode (Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-L -- Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative
